items.title: Beyond "Biomedical-Plus": Reclaiming the biopsychosocial model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy.
items.summary: This article argues that musculoskeletal physiotherapists can move towards a more authentic application of the biopsychosocial (BPS) model by focusing on three interconnected foundational conditions: professional Confidence, role Clarity, and Compassionate communication, to counter the current "biomedical-plus" approach.
items.curatedReason: 生物心理社会模型常被肌肉骨骼物理治疗师简化为“生物医学+”模式。这篇文章直接指出，你需要重审并深入采纳模型核心的“三C”要素：专业自信（Confidence）、角色清晰（Clarity）和同情沟通（Compassionate communication），以更全面地整合情感、关系和社会维度到临床实践中。
items.titleZh: 超越“生物医学+”：肌肉骨骼物理治疗中生物心理社会模型的回归
items.summaryZh: 本文指出，在肌肉骨骼物理治疗中，生物心理社会（BPS）模型常被简化为“生物医学+”的实践。作者认为，物理治疗师可以通过提升专业自信、明确角色定位和运用同情沟通，实现BPS模型的更真实应用，从而更好地整合情感、关系和社会维度。
items.curatedReasonEn: The biopsychosocial model is often simplified to a 'biomedical-plus' approach by MSK physiotherapists. This article directly argues you need to re-evaluate and deeply adopt the model's core '3Cs'—professional Confidence, role Clarity, and Compassionate communication—to more comprehensively integrate emotional, relational, and social dimensions into clinical practice.

items.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
items.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated combined biopsychosocial interventions for chronic low back pain, finding them effective in reducing pain and functional disability in adults (≥18 years) compared to usual care, waiting-list controls, and active unimodal interventions.
items.curatedReason: 这项系统综述和荟萃分析强化了生物心理社会联合干预对慢性腰痛患者的有效性。在与常规治疗、等待观察或单一干预方案相比时，优先考虑多模式的生物心理社会方法，尤其是整合了运动治疗、疼痛教育和心理策略的方案。
items.titleZh: 生物心理社会联合干预对慢性腰痛疗效的系统综述与随机对照试验荟萃分析
items.summaryZh: 一项系统综述和荟萃分析评估了生物心理社会联合干预对慢性腰痛的疗效。研究发现，结合运动治疗、疼痛教育和心理策略的干预方案在减轻成人（≥18岁）慢性腰痛患者的疼痛和功能障碍方面，优于常规治疗、等待观察或单一干预。
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review and meta-analysis reinforces the effectiveness of multimodal biopsychosocial interventions for chronic low back pain. Compared to usual care, waiting list controls, or unimodal interventions, prioritize approaches integrating exercise therapy, pain education, and psychological strategies; they effectively reduce pain and improve functional disability.
items.limitation: 研究结论的普适性可能受限于纳入研究的异质性和干预方案的具体组成。
items.limitationEn: The generalizability of the findings might be limited by the heterogeneity of included studies and specific intervention compositions.
items.journal: Archives of physical medicine and rehabilitation

items.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
items.summary: This systematic review and meta-analysis compared the clinical effects of different non-thrust joint mobilization approaches on objective and subjective outcomes in individuals with shoulder adhesive capsulitis, based on English-language RCTs published between 2000 and 2025.
items.curatedReason: 对于肩关节粘连性囊炎，非冲撞性关节松动术是一个基石干预，但不同手法间的疗效差异此前并不明确。这项荟萃分析将帮助你理解不同关节松动术在缓解疼痛和改善活动度方面的相对优势，从而优化你的临床决策。
items.titleZh: 不同关节松动技术对肩关节粘连性囊炎疗效的比较：一项随机对照试验的系统综述和荟萃分析
items.summaryZh: 这项系统综述和荟萃分析比较了2000年至2025年间发表的英文随机对照试验中，不同非冲撞性关节松动术对肩关节粘连性囊炎患者客观和主观结局的临床效果。
items.curatedReasonEn: For shoulder adhesive capsulitis, non-thrust joint mobilization is a cornerstone intervention. This meta-analysis helps you clarify the relative advantages of different mobilization techniques in pain relief and range of motion improvement, optimizing your clinical decisions. Notably, this is based on English-language RCTs published between 2000 and 2025.
items.limitation: 纳入研究可能存在异质性，影响了不同松动技术之间直接比较的精确性。
items.limitationEn: Potential heterogeneity among included studies may affect the precision of direct comparisons between different mobilization techniques.
items.journal: The Journal of manual & manipulative therapy

items.title: Outpatient rehabilitation can prevent suicide after acquired brain injury.
items.summary: A registry study (n=591 treated, n=2,932 controls) supplemented with medical record reviews found that outpatient rehabilitation programs for adults with acquired brain injuries (ABI) were associated with a lower frequency of suicides compared to a matched control group, suggesting a preventive role.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究提供了重要的观察性证据，表明获得性脑损伤（ABI）患者的门诊康复可能降低自杀发生率。考虑到ABI患者自杀率高于普通人群，这一发现强调了康复服务在整体患者管理中的关键作用，别低估你在患者心理健康维护中的价值。
items.titleZh: 门诊康复可预防获得性脑损伤后的自杀
items.summaryZh: 一项登记研究（治疗组n=591，对照组n=2932），辅以病历回顾，发现接受门诊康复项目的获得性脑损伤（ABI）成人患者，其自杀发生率低于匹配的对照组，提示门诊康复在预防ABI患者自杀中可能发挥作用。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study offers important observational evidence suggesting that outpatient rehabilitation may reduce suicide rates among patients with acquired brain injury (ABI). Given that ABI patients have a higher suicide rate than the general population, this finding underscores the critical role of rehabilitation services in overall patient management; don't underestimate your value in maintaining patients' mental health.
items.limitation: 观察性研究可能存在混杂因素，无法完全排除其他未测量变量对结果的影响。
items.limitationEn: Observational studies may have confounding factors, and cannot fully exclude the influence of other unmeasured variables on the results.

items.title: Exploring equity in telehealth interventions in physiotherapy: A meta-epidemiological study.
items.summary: A meta-epidemiological study (n=94 trials) assessed equity in telehealth interventions in physiotherapy research using the PROGRESS Plus framework, finding that most studies did not adequately report or focus on equity factors.
items.curatedReason: 这项荟萃流行病学研究评估了物理治疗远程医疗干预的公平性，并指出大多数研究未能充分报告或关注PROGRESS Plus框架中的公平性因素。这提醒你，在设计和实施远程医疗项目时，必须积极考虑社会经济、地理和教育等因素，以确保服务的可及性和公平性，别让技术成为新的屏障。
items.titleZh: 探索物理治疗远程医疗干预中的公平性：一项荟萃流行病学研究
items.summaryZh: 一项荟萃流行病学研究（纳入94项试验）使用PROGRESS Plus框架评估了物理治疗远程医疗干预研究中的公平性，发现大多数研究未能充分报告或关注公平性因素。
items.curatedReasonEn: This meta-epidemiological study assessed equity in telerehabilitation interventions for physiotherapy, highlighting that most studies fail to adequately report or focus on equity factors within the PROGRESS Plus framework. This reminds you to actively consider socioeconomic, geographic, and educational factors when designing and implementing telerehabilitation programs to ensure accessibility and equity, and prevent technology from becoming a new barrier.
items.limitation: 研究仅限于2020年至2023年的已发表RCT，可能未能捕捉到更广泛时间范围或非RCT设计的远程医疗实践的公平性特征。
items.limitationEn: The study is limited to RCTs published between 2020 and 2023, and may not capture equity characteristics of telerehabilitation practices in a broader timeframe or with non-RCT designs.

items.title: Digital Addiction and Body Balance: An Investigation of Postural Stability in Young Adults: A Cross-Sectional Study.
items.summary: This cross-sectional study (n=74) investigated postural balance performance in young adults, finding that those with smartphone addiction showed impaired balance, especially when performing attention-demanding tasks, highlighting the impact of digital device use on postural stability.
items.curatedReason: 这项横断面研究提示，过度使用数字设备与年轻人姿势稳定性受损有关，尤其是在执行注意力任务时。这提醒你在评估和治疗年轻患者的平衡问题时，别忘了把数字设备使用情况纳入考量。
items.titleZh: 数字成瘾与身体平衡：一项针对年轻成人姿势稳定性的横断面研究
items.summaryZh: 这项横断面研究（n=74）旨在探讨数字成瘾对年轻成人姿势平衡的影响，发现有智能手机成瘾的年轻人在姿势平衡表现上较差，尤其是在需要注意力分散的任务中，提示数字成瘾与姿势稳定性受损相关。
items.curatedReasonEn: This cross-sectional study suggests that excessive digital device use is linked to impaired postural stability in young adults, especially during attention-demanding tasks. This highlights the importance of considering digital device habits when assessing and treating balance issues in young patients.
items.limitation: 研究为横断面设计，无法建立因果关系，且人群仅限于特定大学的学生。
items.limitationEn: The study's cross-sectional design prevents causal inference, and the population was limited to students from a specific university.
items.journal: Physiotherapy research international : the journal for researchers and clinicians in physical therapy

items.title: Assessment and Management Practices for Post-Stroke Shoulder Pain Among Rehabilitation Professionals in Vietnam: A Cross-Sectional Survey.
items.summary: A cross-sectional survey investigated current assessment and management practices for post-stroke shoulder pain among rehabilitation professionals in 14 Vietnamese hospitals, identifying potential evidence-practice gaps and barriers to effective care.
items.curatedReason: 这项越南的调查揭示了卒中后肩痛评估和管理实践中存在的潜在差距和障碍，尤其是在中低收入国家。这提醒你，在将循证实践应用于不同文化和资源受限的环境时，需要进行本地化调整和考量。
items.titleZh: 越南康复专业人员卒中后肩痛评估与管理实践：一项横断面调查
items.summaryZh: 一项横断面调查研究了越南14家医院康复专业人员在卒中后肩痛评估和管理方面的现有实践，旨在识别潜在的证据与实践差距以及有效护理的障碍。
items.limitation: 研究为横断面调查，仅反映特定地区和时间的实践情况，无法评估干预效果或长期趋势。
items.limitationEn: The study is a cross-sectional survey, reflecting practices only in a specific region and time, and cannot assess intervention effectiveness or long-term trends.
items.journal: Physiotherapy research international : the journal for researchers and clinicians in physical therapy

items.title: Advancing spine connectomics and neural integration through machine learning and neuroengineering: a narrative review.
items.summary: This narrative review summarizes recent advances in spinal connectomics and neural integration, examining how machine learning and neuroengineering are shifting understanding of the spinal cord from a simple reflex relay toward an active system contributing to sensorimotor integration and adaptive motor control.
items.curatedReason: 这篇综述概述了脊髓连接组学在理解脊髓功能上的最新进展，如何从简单的反射通路演变为主动的感觉运动整合系统。了解这些基础神经科学进展有助于你更深层地理解脊髓损伤的神经生理学，虽然它不会直接改变你的临床干预，但拓宽了你对潜在治疗靶点的认知。
items.titleZh: 通过机器学习和神经工程学推进脊髓连接组学和神经整合：一篇叙述性综述
items.summaryZh: 这篇叙述性综述总结了脊髓连接组学和神经整合的最新进展，探讨了机器学习和神经工程学如何将脊髓的理解从简单的反射通路转变为一个有助于感觉运动整合和适应性运动控制的活跃系统。
items.curatedReasonEn: This review outlines recent advances in spinal connectomics, highlighting that the spinal cord is not just a simple reflex relay, but an active system for sensorimotor integration. While it won't directly alter your clinical interventions, it broadens your understanding of spinal cord injury neurophysiology and potential therapeutic targets.
items.journal: European spine journal : official publication of the European Spine Society, the European Spinal Deformity Society, and the European Section of the Cervical Spine Research Society

items.title: The Combined Use of Non-Invasive Neurostimulation with Interactive Digital Technologies in Spinal Cord Injury Rehabilitation: A Scoping Review.
items.summary: A scoping review mapped the empirical evidence (494 unique records identified) regarding the combined use of non-invasive neurostimulation with interactive digital technologies in spinal cord injury rehabilitation, identifying original clinical data from individuals with SCI.
items.curatedReason: 这项范围综述描绘了非侵入性神经刺激与互动数字技术在脊髓损伤康复中结合应用的证据图谱。它有助于你快速了解当前研究的重点和空白，并理解这种组合疗法在临床实践中的潜力与局限性。
items.titleZh: 非侵入性神经刺激与互动数字技术在脊髓损伤康复中联合应用的范围综述
items.summaryZh: 一项范围综述系统地绘制了非侵入性神经刺激与互动数字技术相结合在脊髓损伤康复中的经验证据图谱，识别出494条相关记录，并筛选出报告脊髓损伤患者原创临床数据的研究。
items.curatedReasonEn: This scoping review maps the evidence for combining non-invasive neurostimulation with interactive digital technologies in spinal cord injury rehabilitation. It helps you quickly grasp current research foci and gaps, and understand the potential and limitations of this combined therapy in clinical practice.
items.limitation: 范围综述侧重于证据图谱而非干预效果评估，因此无法提供明确的临床行动建议。
items.limitationEn: The scoping review focuses on mapping evidence rather than evaluating intervention efficacy, so it cannot provide clear clinical action recommendations.

items.title: Effects of multimodal balance training with and without auditory cues on balance, gait mobility, risk of fall and quality of life in patients with chronic stroke
items.summary: This randomized controlled trial (n=42) found that multimodal balance training with auditory cues improved balance, gait mobility, fall risk, and quality of life in chronic stroke survivors aged 45-70 years, compared to training without cues.
items.curatedReason: 这项小型RCT提示，卒中后患者的多模式平衡训练结合听觉线索，可能在改善平衡、步态移动性、跌倒风险和生活质量方面带来额外益处。在为慢性卒中患者设计平衡训练时，可以考虑整合听觉线索以优化效果。
items.titleZh: 多模式平衡训练结合或不结合听觉线索对慢性卒中患者平衡、步态移动性、跌倒风险和生活质量的影响
items.summaryZh: 这项随机对照试验（n=42）发现，对于45-70岁的慢性卒中幸存者，结合听觉线索的多模式平衡训练在改善平衡、步态移动性、跌倒风险和生活质量方面，优于不结合听觉线索的训练。
items.curatedReasonEn: This small RCT suggests that multimodal balance training combined with auditory cues may offer additional benefits in improving balance, gait mobility, fall risk, and quality of life in post-stroke patients. When designing balance training for chronic stroke patients, consider integrating auditory cues to optimize outcomes.
items.limitation: 研究样本量较小（N=42），且为单中心设计，结果的外推性有限。
items.limitationEn: The study has a small sample size (N=42) and is single-center, limiting the generalizability of the results.

items.title: Factors Explaining Limited Daily Hand-use in Individuals with Fair-Good Motor Ability Post-Stroke.
items.summary: This cross-sectional study investigated factors explaining limited daily hand-use in individuals post-stroke with fair-to-good upper extremity motor ability (Fugl-Meyer Assessment >30/66), identifying proprioception, executive functions, self-efficacy, and upper extremity dual-task capacity as key contributors.
items.curatedReason: 卒中后患者即使上肢运动能力良好（Fugl-Meyer Assessment >30/66），日常手部使用仍可能受限。这项研究提示你，评估时需综合考虑本体感觉、执行功能、自我效能和上肢双任务能力，这些是非运动障碍的关键因素，别只盯着肌肉力量和运动控制。
items.titleZh: 卒中后手部运动能力良好患者日常手部使用受限的解释因素
items.summaryZh: 这项横断面研究旨在识别卒中后上肢运动能力（Fugl-Meyer 评估 >30/66）良好但日常手部使用仍受限的解释因素，发现本体感觉、执行功能、自我效能和上肢双任务能力是关键贡献者。
items.curatedReasonEn: Even with fair-to-good upper extremity motor ability (Fugl-Meyer Assessment >30/66) post-stroke, daily hand use can remain limited. This study suggests you must comprehensively consider proprioception, executive functions, self-efficacy, and upper extremity dual-task capacity during assessment; these are key non-motor contributors, so don't focus solely on muscle strength and motor control.
items.limitation: 研究为横断面设计，无法建立各因素与手部使用受限之间的因果关系。
items.limitationEn: The study's cross-sectional design prevents establishing a causal relationship between factors and limited hand use.

items.title: Lifestyle medicine innovations: Physical activity assessment in patient care and implementation of a digital health platform to support healthier movement.
items.summary: This narrative review introduces the ExerciseRxTM digital health platform as a scalable approach to support personalized physical activity and home exercise within clinical care, integrating Physical Activity Vital Sign (PAVS) into an academic healthcare system.
items.curatedReason: 这篇综述介绍了 ExerciseRxTM 数字健康平台及其在临床护理中支持个性化体育活动和家庭锻炼的方法。它提示你关注数字健康工具在物理治疗中的应用潜力，特别是在提高患者依从性和可扩展性方面。
items.titleZh: 生活方式医学创新：患者护理中的体育活动评估与数字健康平台实施以支持更健康运动
items.summaryZh: 这篇叙述性综述介绍了ExerciseRxTM数字健康平台，该平台作为一种可扩展的方法，旨在支持临床护理中的个性化体育活动和家庭锻炼，并将体育活动生命体征（PAVS）整合到学术医疗系统中。
items.curatedReasonEn: This review introduces the ExerciseRxTM digital health platform and its approach to supporting personalized physical activity and home exercise in clinical care. It prompts you to consider the potential of digital health tools in physical therapy, particularly for improving patient adherence and scalability.
items.journal: PM & R : the journal of injury, function, and rehabilitation

items.title: Prediction of Exercise Intolerance in the Recovery Period After Cardiovascular Surgery Using Perioperative Clinical Parameters.
items.summary: This retrospective cohort study (N=590) developed and validated a machine learning model using perioperative clinical parameters to predict reduced exercise capacity at 3 months post-cardiovascular surgery in patients aged ≤75 years.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究开发并验证了一个机器学习模型，用于预测心血管手术后患者3个月的运动能力。这提示你，未来我们可以利用围手术期临床参数更早识别高风险患者，从而更精准地制定心肺康复计划。
items.titleZh: 利用围手术期临床参数预测心血管手术后恢复期运动不耐受
items.summaryZh: 这项回顾性队列研究（N=590）开发并验证了一个机器学习模型，该模型使用围手术期临床参数来预测75岁以下心血管手术患者术后3个月的运动能力下降情况。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study developed and validated a machine learning model to predict exercise capacity at 3 months post-cardiovascular surgery. This suggests that in the future, we can use perioperative clinical parameters to identify high-risk patients earlier, thereby enabling more precise cardiopulmonary rehabilitation planning.
items.limitation: 研究为单中心回顾性队列研究，模型的外推性和普适性可能受限于数据来源。
items.limitationEn: The study is a single-center retrospective cohort study, and the generalizability of the model may be limited by the data source.
items.journal: Archives of physical medicine and rehabilitation

items.title: APTA Members Unite to Advance Payment Advocacy Via New SPARC Forum and Pledge
items.summary: APTA has launched the SPARC Forum and the APTA Payment Advocacy Pledge to empower members in driving meaningful payment reform and strengthening the future of the physical therapy profession, especially regarding payer advocacy in states.
items.curatedReason: APTA推出SPARC论坛和支付倡导承诺，旨在推动支付改革，加强物理治疗师服务。积极参与此类倡导活动，是提升物理治疗行业地位和保障合理报销的关键。
items.titleZh: APTA成员团结起来，通过新的SPARC论坛和承诺推进支付倡导
items.summaryZh: APTA推出了SPARC论坛和APTA支付倡导承诺，旨在赋能成员推动有意义的支付改革，并加强物理治疗专业，尤其是在各州的支付方倡导方面。
items.curatedReasonEn: APTA launched the SPARC Forum and Payment Advocacy Pledge to drive payment reform and strengthen physical therapist services. Actively participating in such advocacy is key to elevating the PT profession and ensuring fair reimbursement.

items.title: Dry Needling State Laws
items.summary: APTA provides an updated infographic (as of April 2026) detailing state laws on dry needling practice by physical therapists in the US, highlighting which states allow, prohibit, or have no specific provisions.
items.curatedReason: APTA提供了各州干针执业法律的最新概况（截至2026年4月）。你在美国执业干针时，务必查阅你所在州的具体法律，确保合规操作。
items.titleZh: 干针执业州法律
items.summaryZh: APTA提供了一份更新至2026年4月的图表，详细说明了美国各州关于物理治疗师干针执业的法律，明确了哪些州允许、禁止或没有具体规定。
items.curatedReasonEn: APTA provides an updated overview of state laws on dry needling practice (as of April 2026). When practicing dry needling in the US, you must consult your state's specific laws to ensure compliant operation.

items.title: Let's talk about sex (and gender) after ACL injury: a ...
items.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis investigated sex and gender differences in self-reported activity and knee-related outcomes after ACL injury, showing distinct patterns between males and females.
items.curatedReason: ACL损伤后，男性和女性在自报活动量和膝关节结局上存在显著差异。你在制定康复方案时，必须考虑这些性别因素，尤其是女性在重返运动和功能结局方面可能面临独特挑战。
items.titleZh: 让我们谈谈ACL损伤后的性别问题：一项关于自报活动和膝关节相关结局的系统综述和荟萃分析
items.summaryZh: 一项系统综述和荟萃分析探讨了ACL损伤后自报活动量和膝关节相关结局的性别差异。
items.curatedReasonEn: Significant sex differences exist in self-reported activity and knee-related outcomes following ACL injury. You must account for these sex-specific factors when developing rehabilitation plans, as females, in particular, may face unique challenges in returning to sport and achieving functional outcomes.
items.limitation: 研究关注的是自报结局，可能存在回忆偏差。
items.limitationEn: The study focuses on self-reported outcomes, which may be subject to recall bias.

items.title: Action observation therapy in pediatric cerebral palsy: an ...
items.summary: An fNIRS-based randomized controlled trial investigated the cortical responses and balance improvements from Action Observation Therapy (AOT) in pediatric cerebral palsy patients, a group of neurodevelopmental disorders affecting movement and posture.
items.curatedReason: 这项RCT探索了儿童脑瘫患者行动观察疗法（AOT）对皮层反应和平衡的影响。如果你在实践中应用AOT，这项研究提供了初步的神经生理学和临床证据。
items.titleZh: 儿童脑瘫行动观察疗法：基于fNIRS的皮层反应和平衡研究——一项随机对照试验
items.summaryZh: 一项基于fNIRS的随机对照试验研究了儿童脑瘫患者行动观察疗法（AOT）对皮层反应和平衡的影响。脑瘫是一组影响运动和姿势的神经发育障碍。
items.curatedReasonEn: This RCT explores the effects of action observation therapy (AOT) on cortical responses and balance in pediatric cerebral palsy patients. If you apply AOT in practice, this study provides preliminary neurophysiological and clinical evidence.
items.limitation: 这是一项初步随机对照试验，可能样本量较小，需更大型研究验证。
items.limitationEn: This is a pilot randomized controlled trial, likely with a small sample size, and requires validation by larger studies.

items.title: Handgrip and leg strength and risk of severe fall injuries in adults aged ≥ 80 years: a prospective HUNT study
items.summary: A prospective HUNT study examined handgrip and leg strength and the risk of severe fall injuries in adults aged ≥80 years, a population underrepresented in fall research, finding reduced muscle strength as a recognized risk factor for falls.
items.curatedReason: 这项前瞻性研究量化了80岁及以上老年人的握力、腿部力量与严重跌倒伤害风险之间的关系。在评估老年患者跌倒风险时，应特别关注其肌肉力量指标。
items.titleZh: 80岁及以上老年人握力、腿部力量与严重跌倒伤害风险：一项前瞻性HUNT研究
items.summaryZh: 一项前瞻性HUNT研究调查了80岁及以上老年人的握力、腿部力量与严重跌倒伤害风险之间的关系。该年龄组在跌倒研究中代表性不足，研究发现肌肉力量减弱是公认的跌倒风险因素。
items.curatedReasonEn: This prospective study quantifies the relationship between handgrip and leg strength and the risk of severe fall injuries in adults aged 80 and above. When assessing fall risk in elderly patients, you should pay particular attention to their muscle strength indicators.
items.limitation: 研究未明确是否控制了所有潜在混杂因素，可能存在残余混杂。
items.limitationEn: The study does not specify whether all potential confounding factors were controlled for, and residual confounding may exist.

items.title: Frailty in patients undergoing video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery for lung cancer: a narrative review of assessment, outcomes and prehabilitation
items.summary: A narrative review summarizes frailty assessment, outcomes, and prehabilitation in patients undergoing video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS) for lung cancer, noting that older adults are increasingly considered for such procedures.
items.curatedReason: 对于肺癌胸腔镜手术患者，虚弱评估应是术前康复的关键环节。请务必在术前识别虚弱个体，并根据这篇综述的建议，制定个性化的预康复方案以优化术后恢复。
items.titleZh: 肺癌胸腔镜手术患者的虚弱评估、结局和术前康复：一项叙述性综述
items.summaryZh: 一篇叙述性综述总结了肺癌胸腔镜手术（VATS）患者的虚弱评估、结局和术前康复，指出老年人越来越多地被考虑进行此类手术。
items.curatedReasonEn: For patients undergoing VATS for lung cancer, frailty assessment must be a critical component of prehabilitation. Ensure you identify frail individuals pre-operatively and, as this review suggests, develop personalized prehabilitation programs to optimize post-operative recovery.
items.limitation: 这是一篇叙述性综述，可能存在选择偏倚，未提供量化证据。
items.limitationEn: This is a narrative review, which may have selection bias and does not provide quantified evidence.

items.title: Development and validation of the pulmonary rehabilitation Kinesiophobia scale for patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
items.summary: This study developed and validated the Pulmonary Rehabilitation Kinesiophobia Scale for patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) in China, as kinesiophobia is a significant barrier to pulmonary rehabilitation.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究开发并验证了COPD患者肺康复运动恐惧症量表。如果你在临床中遇到COPD患者对运动有恐惧，可以使用这个新工具进行评估。
items.titleZh: 慢性阻塞性肺疾病患者肺康复运动恐惧症量表的开发与验证
items.summaryZh: 本研究开发并验证了用于中国慢性阻塞性肺疾病（COPD）患者的肺康复运动恐惧症量表，运动恐惧症是肺康复的一个重要障碍。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study developed and validated the Pulmonary Rehabilitation Kinesiophobia Scale for patients with COPD. If you encounter COPD patients with kinesiophobia in your practice, you can use this new tool for assessment.
items.limitation: 量表在中国COPD患者中开发和验证，其跨文化适用性尚待进一步验证。
items.limitationEn: The scale was developed and validated in Chinese COPD patients, and its cross-cultural applicability needs further validation.

items.title: Stage-specific predictors of exercise adherence after percutaneous coronary intervention: a prospective multicenter study
items.summary: A prospective multicenter study characterized stage-specific predictors of exercise adherence after percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), aiming to address how poor long-term adherence compromises exercise efficacy.
items.curatedReason: PCI术后患者的运动依从性是长期康复成功的关键。这份研究识别了不同阶段的预测因子，这意味着你应根据患者所处的恢复阶段，采取更有针对性的策略来提高其运动依从性。
items.titleZh: 经皮冠状动脉介入治疗后运动依从性的阶段特异性预测因子：一项前瞻性多中心研究
items.summaryZh: 一项前瞻性多中心研究描述了经皮冠状动脉介入治疗（PCI）后运动依从性的阶段特异性预测因子，旨在解决长期依从性差如何影响运动疗效的问题。
items.curatedReasonEn: Exercise adherence is crucial for long-term rehabilitation success after PCI. This study identifies stage-specific predictors, meaning you should implement more targeted strategies to improve exercise adherence based on the patient's recovery phase.
items.limitation: 研究为观察性，无法建立因果关系，预测因子可能受其他未测量因素影响。
items.limitationEn: The study is observational and cannot establish causality; predictors may be influenced by other unmeasured factors.

items.title: Association of Diaphragmatic Crus Thickness Assessed by Computed Tomography with Diaphragmatic Excursion and Exercise Capacity in Patients with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
items.summary: This research explored the association of diaphragmatic crus thickness, assessed by CT, with diaphragmatic excursion and exercise capacity in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), as diaphragmatic dysfunction contributes to exercise intolerance.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究发现COPD患者膈肌脚厚度与膈肌活动度、运动能力相关。在评估COPD患者时，可以关注膈肌影像学指标，这可能提示潜在的运动不耐受。
items.titleZh: CT评估的膈肌脚厚度与慢性阻塞性肺疾病患者膈肌活动度及运动能力的关系
items.summaryZh: 这项研究探讨了通过CT评估的膈肌脚厚度与慢性阻塞性肺疾病（COPD）患者膈肌活动度及运动能力之间的关系，因为膈肌功能障碍会导致运动不耐受。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study found an association between diaphragmatic crus thickness and diaphragmatic excursion and exercise capacity in COPD patients. When assessing COPD patients, you can focus on diaphragmatic imaging indicators, which may suggest potential exercise intolerance.
items.limitation: 研究为横断面，无法确立因果关系，且膈肌脚厚度与运动耐量的具体机制尚需深入研究。
items.limitationEn: The study is cross-sectional, unable to establish causality, and the specific mechanism linking diaphragmatic crus thickness to exercise tolerance requires further in-depth research.

items.title: A systematic review of current evidence on clinical outcomes of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques in sacroiliac joint dysfunction.
items.summary: This systematic review aims to evaluate the clinical efficacy of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques for sacroiliac joint dysfunction (SIJD), searching studies published between January 2021 and December 2025.
items.curatedReason: 如果你在处理骶髂关节功能障碍（SIJD）患者，这份系统综述会帮你梳理腰椎滚动和改良 SIMS 手法治疗的现有证据，指导你未来临床决策。
items.titleZh: 腰椎滚动与改良SIMS手法治疗骶髂关节功能障碍临床结局的系统综述
items.summaryZh: 这项系统综述旨在评估腰椎滚动和改良SIMS手法治疗骶髂关节功能障碍（SIJD）的临床疗效，检索了2021年1月至2025年12月期间发表的研究。
items.curatedReasonEn: If you're treating patients with sacroiliac joint dysfunction (SIJD), this systematic review helps you synthesize current evidence on lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques, guiding your future clinical decisions.
items.limitation: 本系统综述注册在 PROSPERO，但目前尚未提供具体结果，无法评估其最终结论和潜在局限。
items.limitationEn: This systematic review is registered in PROSPERO but has not yet provided specific results, making it impossible to assess its ultimate conclusions and potential limitations.

related.title:
